meta.note: First tungsten-forge pass -- no prior ledger existed. Stage -1 has nothing to read yet; this is the seed.

pass_2.trigger: Manuel's own live USDJPY M5 calibration of SLS(29) completed with a real ghost verdict: STAND DOWN, cause OVERFIT. Full 93.5-minute Journal captured and analysed -- real data, not narrative.

live_calibration_result_verbatim.P1_baseline: WR 34.6%, thr 70, bars 58840, spread 2.4 pips, breakeven 28.6%
live_calibration_result_verbatim.P2_regime_n_and_IC: r0 StrongBull n=8605 IC=0.003 | r1 StrongBear n=11189 IC=0.032 | r2 BullishRev n=748 IC=0.040 | r3 BearishRev n=387 IC=0.040 | r4 Range n=140 IC=0.018 | r5 Choppy n=4415 IC=0.037 | r6 Noise n=3 IC=0.000 | r7 BuildingMom n=5223 IC=0.008 | r8 ControlledPullback n=150 IC=0.008
live_calibration_result_verbatim.P3_weight_search: Total combos searched: 562,500 in the initial global sweep. 0/9 regimes beat 60% WR on 30% holdout on the first attempt; target relaxed to 55%, still every regime ultimately COMPLETE'd as 'no edge found -> equal weights' after enh-check flagged linear-vs-enhanced divergence as overfit in every case (fit-check gaps: -19pp to -44pp train-to-holdout, universally)
live_calibration_result_verbatim.P5_prelaunch: only 17 ghost trades (need 30+), logged as best-effort, not a hard fail
live_calibration_result_verbatim.P6_ghost: OOS window 6048 bars, only ~1 qualifying trade at any threshold tried (70 then 50) -- ghostWR 0.0%, PF 2.00, EV 0.0000 (need 0.006), train 34.6% -> OOS 0.0% (-34.6pp), WF flagged FRAGILE, 3/6 gates passed
live_calibration_result_verbatim.final_verdict: CALIBRATION RESULT: STAND DOWN, cause OVERFIT. LIVE: STOOD DOWN -- edge below viability, observing only, no risk taken. ARMED save skipped (validation failed). Fresh recalibration required on next start.

pass_2.diagnosis: The STAND DOWN itself is CORRECT, not a bug -- Stage 10's own language: a ghost refusal is the architecture working. Every regime independently showing the identical shape (good train WR, holdout collapse of -19 to -44pp, enh-check correctly detecting overfit, correctly falling back to equal weights) is not 9 separate failures -- it is the SAME underlying methodology issue expressed 9 times. The `Total combos: 562500` figure is the real lead: searching 562,500 weight combinations against a single train/holdout split, with no multiple-testing correction sized to that count and no walk-forward across multiple non-overlapping windows (SS-III.6's own requirement), is close to a textbook overfitting generator. The existing enh-check/fit-check safety nets are correctly catching the SYMPTOM every time (hence equal-weight fallback, hence the correct STAND DOWN) -- but the search itself doesn't know how much fishing it did.
pass_2.action_taken: NOT attempting to force a different (trading) outcome out of this data -- doing so would violate Standing Prohibition #2 ('never ship a change that has not beaten an orthogonal metric') and the thesis's own central point that absence of edge is a valid, correct answer. Fixed instead: (1) WARRANT#9, the n<500 Power Doctrine floor (hypothesis #2 from pass 1) -- live evidence directly confirms its necessity, since regimes r3/r4/r8 (n=387/140/150, all <500) were computing and shrinking ICs the Power Doctrine's own MDE table says cannot resolve anything but an absurd edge; (2) Lesson#4, the SLS(24) hardcoded version-string bug -- fixed via new EA_BUILD_ID constant, all 8 sites now derive from one place.
pass_2.deferred_not_fixed: The 562,500-combo multiple-testing gap in Phase 3B's weight search is NOT fixed this pass -- redesigning that search's correction/walk-forward methodology is a substantially larger undertaking (needs its own warrant with a numeric prediction of how the correction changes acceptance rates, plus validation that it doesn't just make every regime permanently equal-weight) and risks being a bigger, riskier change than this pass should batch alongside WARRANT#9. Logged as hypothesis #3 for a future pass.

compile_result.method: metaeditor.exe /compile via PowerShell Start-Process -- bash/MSYS mangled the /compile: argument silently (exit 0, no log, no .ex4 produced) on two attempts before switching tools; root-caused with a paren-free filename test, confirmed not a parentheses/quoting issue but the bash-to-native-exe argument path specifically
compile_result.result: 0 errors, 1 warnings, 618 msec elapsed -- the single warning ('possible use of uninitialized variable sesMap') is pre-existing, identical to the SLS(24) build's own compile log, not introduced by this pass's edits
compile_result.not_done: Not yet attached to a live/demo chart or backtested -- compiling clean proves the code is syntactically sound, not that WARRANT#9's predicted effect (r3/r4/r8 now retaining prior instead of computing a doomed IC) actually holds. That still needs a real calibration run.

pass_3.trigger: Five-message directive stack received via the relay mailbox (Grand Mandate + Win-Intelligence engine, the nine-lens Edge Doctrine, a reverse-mining addendum, a win-rate/read-quality correction, and a final doctrine adding retrospective intelligence). TUNGSTEN_EDGE_DOCTRINE.md installed at ~/.claude/skills/tungsten-forge/EDGE_DOCTRINE.md as the mail instructed. Base build verified byte-identical (content-wise; only CRLF line-ending differences) to the already-compiled SLS(30) -- no drift from the mailed attachment.
pass_3.scope_decision: The mail's combined mandate (win-intelligence extraction/sizing/ensemble/meta-learning/live-recognition engines, nine lenses of edge, a reverse-mining discovery engine, and a six-question retrospective-intelligence reflex) is an enormous, multi-pass build -- attempting all of it in one pass would violate SS-X's 'never batch subsystems' rule and this project's own established practice (pass 1 = threshold-legality sweep only, pass 2 = one power-floor fix only). Rather than write narrative/unvalidated code toward the full mandate, this pass picked ONE concrete, well-scoped, real defect directly in the spirit of the mail's Lens 1 (Edge as Timing / session-conditioning) and found it was not a green-field build: TUNGSTEN already has a mature session-conditioning subsystem (Phase 3B.5, 4 sessions x 9 regimes) and a volatility-conditioning sibling (Phase 3B.6, 3 vol-states x 9 regimes). The real defect was that WARRANT#9's n>=500 Power Doctrine floor, shipped in pass 2, was never propagated to either of these two sibling subsystems.
pass_3.diagnosis: OptimizeSessionWeightsPerRegime (Phase 3B.5, ~line 5894) gated its coordinate-descent weight fit at `sCnt < 20` and accepted the fitted result over the regime-level prior whenever `finalTrades>=30 && WR_delta>=2.0pp` -- both drastically below the n>=500 floor SS-III.4 makes binding ('a cell with n<500 may not update a weight, ever, on any evidence'). OptimizeVolatilityWeightsPerRegime (Phase 3B.6, ~line 6062) was worse: it runs a 4-stage grid search (Stages A-D, up to ~470,000 weight-combination evaluations total) against a cell that could hold as few as `vCnt=20` bars, and marks the result `validated=true` unconditionally the moment any candidate beats the initial -1 composite score -- no WR-delta acceptance bar at all. Both subsystems' outputs are live-consequential, not diagnostic-only: `g_SessionWeightsValid[ses][regime]` directly relieves the live entry threshold by 2.0 points (line ~8091) and both session/volatility weight sets get blended into the live composite score (lines ~14668-14669, ~33569-33574). This is the exact same overfit mechanism that produced the real STAND DOWN/OVERFIT verdict analysed in pass 2 (Phase 3B's 562,500-combo search on one train/holdout split) -- just unpatched in two sibling subsystems that WARRANT#9 never touched, because pass 2 scoped itself to the main per-regime IC pipeline only.
pass_3.action_taken: WARRANT#10: raised OptimizeSessionWeightsPerRegime's floor from `sCnt<20` to `sCnt<500` (skip gate at line ~5989) and its validity check from `sCnt>=20` to `sCnt>=500` (line ~6064). WARRANT#11: same fix for OptimizeVolatilityWeightsPerRegime, `vCnt<20`->`vCnt<500` (skip gate ~6154) and `vCnt>=20`->`vCnt>=500` (validity check ~6228). Both are minimal, mechanical threshold-only edits mirroring WARRANT#9's own precedent exactly -- no change to the coordinate-descent/grid-search logic itself, the acceptance-rule secondary checks, the session/vol-state boundary definitions, or any downstream consumer. Version bumped 9.44->9.45, EA_BUILD_ID SLS(30)->SLS(31), header banner updated with a dated changelog entry.
pass_3.deferred_not_fixed: Hypothesis #3 (the Phase 3B primary weight-search's own 562,500-combo multiple-testing/walk-forward gap, logged pass 2) is STILL not fixed -- this pass fixed two sibling subsystems that share the same *symptom* (accepting underpowered fits) but not the primary search's *specific* methodology gap (no correction sized to combo count, no multi-window walk-forward). Also NOT started this pass, and explicitly logged as roadmap rather than silently dropped, is the entire mail mandate beyond this one defect class: the Win-Intelligence engine (extraction-geometry search W-I.1, growth-optimal sizing W-I.2, deeper conditional-edge discovery W-I.3, edge ensemble W-I.4, meta-learning W-I.5, live opportunity recognition W-I.6), the remaining eight Edge Doctrine lenses (L2 exit, L3 avoidance, L4 asymmetry, L5 survival, L6 opportunity cost, L7 emergence, L8 adaptation speed, L9 flow/forced-participant reframe -- L1 timing is partially served by this pass's fix, since a session edge that could never replicate at n=20 now can't silently masquerade as validated), the reverse-mining discovery engine, and the six-question retrospective-intelligence reflex (Addendum B). See `mandate_roadmap_2026_07_10` below for the full un-collapsed list, so a future pass reads this instead of re-deriving scope from the raw mail text.

hypotheses.statement: Does ClassifyBarToRegime_Core's hard gate (line 32532) and the s8 soft-score mirror (line 3157) still use the same moveSig-derived threshold, or have they diverged as the mail's own audit describes as a past defect class?
hypotheses.prereg_test: direct source comparison of both blocks' threshold-derivation lines
hypotheses.prereg_n_required: n/a -- static code comparison, not a statistical test
hypotheses.measured_effect: IDENTICAL: both use `g_ART.calibrated ? g_ART.moveSig : 0.002` verbatim (hard gate at line 32544, soft mirror at line 3170). A 'SLS27-CLAUDE COHERENCE FIX' comment at the hard gate confirms this was deliberately unified in a prior pass, before this forge run began.
hypotheses.note: the mail's example of this defect class was already fixed by prior work, not a defect discovered in this pass. Residual risk noted separately: the same formula is still written out twice in two places rather than hoisted to one accessor (SKILL.md V's own enforcement recommendation), so a future edit to only one site would silently reintroduce the divergence. Not urgent (currently correct), flagged for a future hoist-to-accessor warrant rather than fixed opportunistically now.

hypotheses.subsystem: Per-regime IC computation / power gate (Stage 4, composition order SS-VI)
hypotheses.statement: Does the actual per-regime IC pipeline (shrink -> mirror-pool -> 70/30 persistence blend, the code around lines 4090-4280) enforce the Power Doctrine's binding floor -- 'a cell with n<500 may not update a weight, ever' (SS-III.4 rule 3, Standing Prohibition #5) -- before an IC is allowed to affect live weights?
hypotheses.prereg_test: grep for a 500-sample gate anywhere between IC computation and weight persistence; read the actual skip-condition at the top of the per-regime loop
hypotheses.predicted_effect: unknown before checking -- this is exactly the class of gap SS-III (Power Doctrine, marked 'new in v2.0') was written to catch, so plausibly still present since the file's own IC/shrinkage code predates v2.0 (SLS26/27/28-CLAUDE comments, not SLS29-FORGE)
hypotheses.measured_effect: CONFIRMED GAP: the only power-related gate found is `if(n < 30) continue;` at line 4104 -- 16x more permissive than the mandated 500. Shrinkage (n>6), mirror-pooling eligibility (n>=30 per side), and the 70/30 persistence blend all run on any regime with n>=30, with NO n>=500 check anywhere between IC computation and it being written into g_RegimeIC / g_RegimeICCcy (which downstream weight derivation reads). At n=30-499 the skill's own MDE table (SS-III.3) says the cell cannot resolve anything but an absurd effect (MDE 0.196-0.309R) -- yet its IC still gets shrunk, possibly pooled, and blended into persistent live weights every calibration.
hypotheses.note: Fixed in pass 2, triggered by real live evidence: the 2026-07-09 USDJPY M5 calibration's own P2 log showed regimes r3/r4/r8 at n=387/140/150 (all <500) computing and shrinking real ICs. Gate raised from n<30 to n<500 in TUNGSTEN_9.44_A7.5_QUANT_SLS(30).mq4. Downstream mirror-pooling's own n<30-per-side check is now unreachable for anything under 500 (those regimes never set _icMeasured=true), so no cascading edit was needed there. NOT yet backtest-validated against a fresh calibration -- prediction: r3/r4/r8 should now retain prior/default (0.05, equal weight) instead of computing a doomed-to-overfit IC, with no change to r0/r1/r2/r5/r7 (all already >=500).

hypotheses.statement: Does the live-observed universal overfit pattern (every one of 9 regimes: good train WR, holdout collapse -19 to -44pp) trace to a specific, fixable methodology gap, or is it simply correct evidence of no real edge on this instrument?
hypotheses.prereg_test: read the Phase 3 log's own reported combo count and compare against SS-III.6's pre-registration/correction requirements
hypotheses.prereg_n_required: n/a -- diagnostic read of live log output
hypotheses.predicted_effect: if a methodology gap exists, expect it to show as an uncorrected multiple-testing exposure (search breadth >> what a single train/holdout split can validate)
hypotheses.measured_effect: Phase 3 logged 'Total combos: 562500' tested against a single train/holdout split, with no walk-forward across multiple non-overlapping windows (SS-III.6's own requirement) and no correction sized to that combo count. This is present identically across all 9 regimes' results -- consistent with one shared root cause rather than 9 independent instrument-specific non-findings.
hypotheses.verdict: PLAUSIBLE, not CONFIRMED -- the existing enh-check/fit-check safety nets already catch and correctly neutralise the symptom every time (equal-weight fallback, correct STAND DOWN), so this is not a correctness failure in the sense of bad live behaviour. It is a methodology inefficiency: the search spends a lot of compute (93.5 min budget, mostly Phase 3B) generating combinations that are ~guaranteed to be rejected.
hypotheses.status: STILL DEFERRED -- not fixed pass 3 either
hypotheses.note: Redesigning Phase 3B's search (proper k-fold or multi-window walk-forward, or a combo-count-aware acceptance bar) is a substantially larger undertaking than a threshold-only fix and risks batching multiple subsystems in one pass (SS-X's own prohibition). Pass 3 fixed the *sibling* subsystems (3B.5, 3B.6 -- see hypothesis #4/warrants #10-#11) that share the underpowered-acceptance symptom, but the primary Phase 3B search's own combo-count/walk-forward gap named here is untouched. Needs its own warrant with a numeric prediction of how correction changes acceptance rates, and validation that it does not simply make every regime permanently equal-weight regardless of instrument. Still the leading candidate for a future pass's single scope.

hypotheses.subsystem: Session (3B.5) and volatility (3B.6) weight-refinement power gates
hypotheses.statement: Given WARRANT#9 fixed the n<500 floor in the main per-regime IC pipeline, do the session-conditioned (Phase 3B.5) and volatility-conditioned (Phase 3B.6) weight-refinement subsystems -- introduced as separate, parallel search loops -- enforce the same floor, or does the same class of gap exist unpatched in a sibling subsystem WARRANT#9 never touched?
hypotheses.prereg_test: read both OptimizeSessionWeightsPerRegime and OptimizeVolatilityWeightsPerRegime for their sample-size skip gate and their weight-validity acceptance condition; trace forward to every consumer of g_SessionWeightsValid/g_VolatilityWeightsValid to establish blast radius
hypotheses.prereg_n_required: n/a -- static code read, same method as hypothesis #2
hypotheses.predicted_effect: given hypothesis #2's own finding that a mature, complex file can carry multiple independent occurrences of the same defect class, predicted a comparable gap was plausible in these sibling subsystems even though WARRANT#9 fixed the primary pipeline
hypotheses.measured_effect: CONFIRMED GAP, worse than hypothesis #2 in one dimension: Phase 3B.5 gated at `sCnt<20` (skip) / `sCnt>=20` (validity), accepting a fit whenever finalTrades>=30 and WR delta>=2.0pp -- both far below n=500. Phase 3B.6 gated identically at `vCnt<20`/`vCnt>=20`, but runs a 4-stage grid search of up to ~470,000 weight-combination evaluations per context against that same tiny cell, and marks the result validated unconditionally on any improvement over the initial -1 composite -- no WR-delta bar at all, i.e. weaker acceptance discipline than the session subsystem it sits beside. Blast radius confirmed live-consequential, not diagnostic: g_SessionWeightsValid gates a direct -2.0pt live entry-threshold relief (line ~8091) and both flags gate which weight set (fitted vs regime-level prior) blends into the live composite score (lines ~14668-14669, ~33569-33574).
hypotheses.verdict: CONFIRMED -- real, current defect, same class as hypothesis #2, different subsystem
hypotheses.note: Fixed by raising both subsystems' skip gate and validity-check threshold from the cell's raw sample count (20) to 500, mirroring WARRANT#9's precedent exactly. NOT yet backtest-validated against a fresh calibration -- prediction: on EURUSD/USDJPY M5, only the largest regimes' largest session/vol-state sub-buckets (a fraction of r0/r1/r5/r7, which have regime-level n in the thousands) should still clear n>=500 after a 4-way or 3-way split; every other session x regime and vol-state x regime cell should now retain the regime-level prior (g_SessionWeightsValid/g_VolatilityWeightsValid = false) instead of fitting/accepting a coordinate-descent or grid-search result on n=20-100 bars.

lessons.believed: Point*5/Point*10 style constants are harmless small buffers
lessons.observed: on a 5-digit EURUSD broker, Point*5 is 0.5 pip and Point*10 is 1 pip -- several in-code comments (minTolerance 'At least 5 pips', pipDist named as if 10 pips) were written for a 4-digit convention and silently mean 10x less than the author believed
lessons.updated: any raw Point*N site must be checked against the comment's stated intent, not just converted mechanically -- the comment itself can be evidence of a live bug, not just documentation
lessons.generality: INSTRUMENT-SPECIFIC (digit convention), but the class (SCALE, comment/value mismatch) is UNIVERSAL
lessons.guards: Stage 1 static sweep now greps every Point*N site and cross-checks nearby comments for pip-count claims

lessons.believed: ema21Angle's hardcoded degree cut-points (10.0/10.0/12.0) were three independent tuning choices
lessons.observed: the angle itself is already correctly ATR-normalised (Form-3 legal) at its point of computation, but the three consuming sites never agreed with each other (10 vs 10 vs 12) and none were derived from data -- exactly the OPEN item the mail itself flagged
lessons.updated: a dimensionless/legal quantity can still carry an illegal threshold applied to it downstream; legality of the value and legality of the cut-point are separate audits
lessons.confidence: MEDIUM (single pass, not yet orthogonally validated -- that's Stage 7's job)
lessons.guards: new g_ART.ema21AngleTrend field, learned per-instrument via ART_CalibrateStructural, same idiom as emaDispTrend/emaSlopeTrend

lessons.believed: spread/ATR could be measured from the stored M5 history like everything else in Stage 0
lessons.observed: every stored spread field in the 121,276-bar .hst is 0 -- MT4 does not backfill live spread into historical bars
lessons.updated: spread cost must come from live MarketInfo(MODE_SPREAD) sampling or tick history, never from static .hst -- and Stage 0 must say so rather than reporting a false zero
lessons.generality: UNIVERSAL (applies to any MT4 instrument profiled this way)
lessons.guards: stage0_profile.json carries median_spread_over_ATR=null with an explicit UNMEASURABLE note instead of 0.0

lessons.believed: the EA's own Journal/Print output identifies which SLS build produced it
lessons.observed: every LogCriticalEvent/PrintFormat/Print version banner in the file (lines 19510, 20539, 28916, 29124, 41342, 47954, 48047, 48050, and others) hardcodes the literal string "SLS(24)" regardless of the file's actual identity -- confirmed live on 2026-07-09: a genuinely-recompiled SLS(29).ex4 (different binary from SLS(24).ex4, verified via cmp) still printed "TUNGSTEN 9.43 A7.5 QUANT SLS(24)" in its own Journal log throughout a real calibration run. Only the file-header banner comment (line 119) and the .ex4/chart filename were ever updated release-over-release; the runtime-visible identity string was not.
lessons.updated: never trust the EA's own printed version string to identify which build produced a log -- confirm via .ex4 byte comparison or compile timestamp instead. This is exactly the VII.1/VII.2 'Voice' failure mode the skill warns about (a log that doesn't say what it actually is), just applied to identity rather than a learned value.
lessons.generality: UNIVERSAL (any file that hardcodes a version literal instead of deriving it once)
lessons.guards: single EA_BUILD_ID constant substituted into every Print site, defined once near the file header, bumped each pass. FIXED pass 2.

lessons.believed: fixing a Power Doctrine gap in one subsystem (the main per-regime IC pipeline, WARRANT#9) closed the class of defect for the file
lessons.observed: two sibling subsystems (Phase 3B.5 session weights, Phase 3B.6 volatility weights) that were never in WARRANT#9's blast radius carried the identical gap independently -- same underpowered-cell-updates-a-live-weight pattern, different code path entirely, one of them (3B.6) with an even weaker acceptance rule than the pipeline WARRANT#9 fixed
lessons.updated: a Standing Prohibition is a property of the SYSTEM, not of the one code path where it was first found broken -- fixing an instance does not fix the class. Whenever a Power Doctrine (or any Standing Prohibition) gap is found and fixed, the fix's own commit should trigger a grep for structurally similar patterns (same skip-gate idiom, same 'if n < threshold: retain prior' shape) elsewhere in the file, not just close the one warrant.
lessons.confidence: HIGH -- directly observed twice now (WARRANT#9's fix left #10/#11 undiscovered until pass 3 went looking deliberately)
lessons.guards: recommend a Stage 1-style periodic full-file grep for `< 30`, `< 20`, or similar small-integer sample-size gates near any weight/threshold assignment, tracked as its own recurring check rather than a one-time sweep -- not yet implemented as an automated check, flagged for a future pass

prediction.fire_rate: double-bottom/top detection rate comparable pre/post on EURUSD M5

warrants.blast_radius: marketStructure.trendAngles[0..2] and maSlopes[0..9] -- consumed wherever marketStructure angle/slope fields are read (PARITY concern: a second, separately-normalised angle exists alongside the ATR-normalised ema21Angle/ema8Angle)
warrants.defect: denominator hardcoded 5*Point instead of ATR-normalised -- SCALE class, and diverges from the correctly-normalised angle calc elsewhere in the same file (two ways to compute 'trend angle')
warrants.intervention: 5*Point -> freshly-fetched _atrTA/_atrMA * 5 in each function (mirrors the _atrA*_N idiom from the legal ema21Angle block)

prediction.forward_R: not directly tradeable-affecting unless marketStructure.trendAngles/maSlopes feed a live gate -- consumer audit not yet done, flagged for Stage 3

warrants.falsifier: if a downstream consumer of trendAngles/maSlopes shows behavior change beyond the SCALE fix's intent
warrants.decision_rule: ship iff structural parity (H6) holds and no consumer regression found in Stage 3 consumer audit

warrants.subsystem: ART_CalibrateStructural + 3 consumers (s8 scoring, R8 exhaustion, confirm-vote)
warrants.blast_radius: every consumer of features.ema21Angle threshold: line 3177 (s8 score), line 32557 (R8 exhaustion), line 32731 (confirm votes) -- named exactly as the mail's own open item
warrants.defect: three un-agreeing hardcoded degree cut-points (10.0, 10.0, 12.0) on an already-ATR-normalised angle -- the cut-point itself was never learned (mail's own PART IX.1: 'its 12 deg cut-point is still Form-1 material and remains OPEN')
warrants.evidence: measured on 121,026 real EURUSD M5 bars: p93 of |ema21Angle| = 10.538 deg (script: stage0_profile + inline calc), which is where the old 10/12 deg cuts actually sit on this instrument (~p92-95) -- so this conversion preserves current behaviour rather than re-tuning it (that's Stage 3's job, not Stage 2's)
warrants.intervention: added g_ART.ema21AngleTrend field + learning block in ART_CalibrateStructural (same bar walk, no extra pass, mirrors emaDispTrend/emaSlopeTrend idiom exactly); all 3 consumers now read g_ART.ema21AngleTrend instead of a literal

prediction.orthogonal_discrimination: unknown until Stage 7 -- this pass only guarantees Form-1 legality + preserved current fire-rate, not improved discrimination
prediction.fire_rate: expected ~unchanged on EURUSD M5 at calibration time (p93 chosen specifically to match old behavior)
prediction.calibration_runtime_delta: +1 percentile sort of a maxBars-length array per calibration, same order of cost as existing dispArr/slopeArr sorts

warrants.falsifier: if backtested fire-rate of any of the 3 consumers shifts >15% relative post-change, the p93 choice diverged more than intended from the old fixed cuts
warrants.decision_rule: ship iff fire-rate delta < 15% relative on all 3 consumers (Stage 3/6 backtest, not yet run)

warrants.blast_radius: g_RegimeIC / g_RegimeICCcy for every regime, every calibration -- feeds all downstream weight derivation
warrants.defect: power-gate floor enforced at n<30, 16x more permissive than Standing Prohibition #5's n<500
warrants.evidence: hypothesis #2 (pass 1 static read), directly confirmed as live-consequential by the 2026-07-09 USDJPY M5 calibration's own P2 log (regimes r3/r4/r8 at n=387/140/150, all <500, computing real ICs)
warrants.intervention: if(n < 30) continue; -> if(n < 500) continue; (single-line threshold change, no downstream cascading edit needed since mirror-pooling's own n>=30-per-side check becomes unreachable below 500)

prediction.forward_R: r3/r4/r8 should retain prior (0.05, equal weight) instead of computing a doomed-to-overfit IC; no change expected for r0/r1/r2/r5/r7 (already >=500)
prediction.fire_rate: n/a -- this is a weight-update gate, not an entry gate
prediction.calibration_runtime_delta: expected slightly negative (less IC computation work for now-skipped thin regimes)

warrants.falsifier: if a fresh calibration still shows r3/r4/r8 computing a non-default IC, the fix did not take (check .ex4 byte-diff against SLS(29))
warrants.decision_rule: ship iff compiles clean AND (pending) fresh calibration confirms r3/r4/r8 retain prior

warrants.blast_radius: g_SessionRegimeWeights[4][9] / g_SessionWeightsValid[4][9] for every session x regime context, every calibration; consumed at line ~8091 (direct -2.0pt live entry-threshold relief when valid), line ~14668 (live composite-score blend), lines ~24352/24750/24952 (persistence save/load), line ~33569 (blend-ratio calibration), lines ~35321/~40714/~40733 (diagnostics/coverage logging)
warrants.defect: power-gate floor enforced at sCnt<20 (skip) / sCnt>=20 (validity), 25x more permissive than Standing Prohibition #5's n>=500; secondary acceptance rule (finalTrades>=30, WR delta>=2.0pp) is not a substitute for the floor since it operates on the same undersized sample
warrants.evidence: hypothesis #4 (pass 3 static read + downstream consumer trace), same defect class as hypothesis #2/WARRANT#9 but in a subsystem WARRANT#9 never touched
warrants.intervention: sCnt<20 -> sCnt<500 (skip gate, line ~5989); sCnt>=20 -> sCnt>=500 (validity check, line ~6064). No change to the coordinate-descent search itself, the WR-delta secondary acceptance rule, or any consumer.

prediction.forward_R: most of the 36 session x regime contexts should now retain the regime-level prior; only the largest regimes' (r0/r1/r5/r7, regime-level n in the thousands) largest sessions should still clear 500 after a 4-way split -- prediction: 4-8 of 36 contexts remain valid post-fix, down from an unmeasured but presumably much higher pre-fix count (every context with sCnt>=20 could previously validate)
prediction.fire_rate: n/a -- this is a weight-update/threshold-relief gate, not an entry gate directly, but the -2.0pt threshold relief at line ~8091 should now fire far less often
prediction.calibration_runtime_delta: expected negative -- the 150ms-budgeted coordinate descent (up to 84 evaluations) no longer runs for any now-skipped underpowered context

warrants.falsifier: if a fresh calibration shows more than a handful of the 36 session x regime contexts still validating, either a regime's session distribution is far more uniform than assumed or the fix did not take (check .ex4 byte-diff against SLS(30))
warrants.decision_rule: ship iff compiles clean AND (pending) fresh calibration confirms the large majority of session x regime contexts now retain prior

warrants.blast_radius: g_VolatilityRegimeWeights[3][9] / g_VolatilityWeightsValid[3][9] for every vol-state x regime context, every calibration; consumed at line ~14669 (live composite-score blend), lines ~24366/24763/24964 (persistence save/load), line ~33574 (blend-ratio calibration)
warrants.defect: power-gate floor enforced at vCnt<20 (skip) / vCnt>=20 (validity) guarding a 4-stage, up-to-~470,000-combination grid search (Stages A-D); acceptance sets validated=true unconditionally on any improvement over the initial -1 composite -- no WR-delta bar at all, weaker than the sibling session subsystem's own (insufficient) acceptance rule
warrants.evidence: hypothesis #4 (pass 3 static read + downstream consumer trace)
warrants.intervention: vCnt<20 -> vCnt<500 (skip gate, line ~6154); vCnt>=20 -> vCnt>=500 (validity check, line ~6228). No change to the 4-stage grid search itself or any consumer.

prediction.forward_R: most of the 27 vol-state x regime contexts should now retain the regime-level prior; only the largest regimes' largest vol-state buckets should still clear 500
prediction.calibration_runtime_delta: expected substantially negative -- this is the single most expensive skipped computation of the two warrants, since the pre-fix code ran up to ~470,000 weight evaluations per underpowered context

warrants.falsifier: if a fresh calibration shows more than a handful of the 27 vol-state x regime contexts still validating, or if calibration runtime does not drop noticeably in Phase 3B.6, the fix did not take
warrants.decision_rule: ship iff compiles clean AND (pending) fresh calibration confirms the large majority of vol-state x regime contexts now retain prior AND Phase 3B.6 wall-clock time drops

competence_vector_pass_3.coverage: 2 new gates added to the thresholds table (session/volatility power floors), both converted; the file's threshold coverage is still not exhaustive -- pass 3 did not re-run a full Stage 1 static sweep, it followed a specific lead (hypothesis #4) rather than a breadth-first audit
competence_vector_pass_3.legality: n/a for this pass's warrants -- POWER_FLOOR is not one of the three Form-1/2/3 legality classes (SS-IX.1), it is a Standing Prohibition #5 enforcement gate; both are now correctly binding at n=500
competence_vector_pass_3.reachability: not yet measured -- same as every prior pass's warrants, requires a real calibration run
competence_vector_pass_3.power_honesty: this entire pass IS a power-honesty fix (propagating the n>=500 floor to two subsystems that lacked it) -- but note the irony flagged in Lesson#5: pass 2's own power-honesty fix (WARRANT#9) was itself incomplete, having missed these two siblings. Coverage of 'is every weight-update gate in this file power-honest' is still not proven exhaustive after 3 passes.
competence_vector_pass_3.prediction_MAE: STILL N/A -- three passes in and zero warrants (of 11 total) have been measured against a real calibration/backtest yet. Every single one remains PENDING or SHIPPED_..._NOT_YET_CALIBRATION_VALIDATED. This is the single most important gap in the project's own self-assessment: SS-II.4's prediction-scoring engine cannot report an MAE, CI coverage, or directional accuracy because the loop has never closed on a real measurement.
competence_vector_pass_3.regression_rate: 0/11 shipped-and-reverted, still not yet meaningful per SS-II.5 (nothing has been tested against reality to revert from)
competence_vector_pass_3.discovery_yield: 1 new hypothesis (id 4) found this pass from a targeted read of 2 functions (not a full-file sweep) -- both sub-findings (WARRANT#10, #11) confirmed real. Yield per function read is high, suggesting more of the same defect class likely exists elsewhere in the file's other Phase-3-family functions (3B.7 blend-ratio calibration was read and found structurally different -- alpha-blend grid, not per-cell weight fit -- and not flagged as the same defect, but was not exhaustively audited either).

stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_0_instrument_profiling: DONE -- real 121,026-bar EURUSD M5 profile, one metric honestly UNMEASURABLE (spread)
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_1_reachability_clamp_audit: DONE (static) -- 18 gates found, cross-referenced to real percentiles
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_2_threshold_legality: DONE -- 8/8 real offenders converted, 8 warrants filed, structural parity (H6) verified, no compile (held per instruction)
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_3_resurrection: PARTIAL -- IMPULSE_BREAK and Regime-8 were already resurrected in prior passes (SLS27/28-CLAUDE), verified still present and correct in SLS(29); no NEW dead branches found in this pass's static sweep. Real fire-rate comparability across M5/M15/H1/H4 NOT measured -- requires backtest, not done this pass.
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_4_learning_integrity: PARTIAL -- composition order (shrink -> mirror-pool -> 70/30 blend) verified to match SS-VI's sequence in the actual code. CONFIRMED a real, unfixed gap: the n<500 power-gate floor (Standing Prohibition #5) is not enforced anywhere in the IC pipeline -- only n<30. Logged as hypothesis #2, status PENDING_WARRANT, deliberately not fixed opportunistically (needs its own warrant).
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_5_hardening_sweep: PARTIAL -- H6 (structural parity) done in Stage 2. H4 (silent-failure ban) spot-checked: 248 brace-less `if(!g_CalibSilent)` sites exist; confirmed this is normal single-statement MQL4 style, not inherently the dangling-amputation bug the skill warns about, but a full pairwise check of all 248 for the specific 'second statement should have been inside the conditional' pattern was NOT done -- out of scope for this pass, flagged for later. H7 (persistence markers) spot-checked: 0xC59A/0xD520/0xE210 write/read guards all match correctly. H3/H5/H8 not audited this pass.
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_6_parity_null_ghost: PARTIAL -- H9 spot-check done (regime-8 hard-gate/soft-mirror, hypothesis #1, CONFIRMED consistent). Null-ghost surrogate testing (H10) and the 500-bar live-sync replay NOT done -- both require an actual Strategy Tester/ghost run, which needs the live calibration that was interrupted (see below) to actually complete.

stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_8_ghost_gate: NOT DONE -- requires a completed calibration + holdout ghost run.
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_9_close_ledger: THIS ENTRY. Pass 1 is being closed now on the user's explicit instruction to finish, with stages 3/4/5/6 honestly partial and 7/8 not started, rather than continue waiting indefinitely for the live USDJPY M5 calibration (interrupted once already by my own tooling, not yet successfully restarted/completed as of this entry).
stage_disposition_pass_1.stage_10_hold: Demo only, unchanged -- nothing here ever touched a live/funded account. No live-attach decision to make; none of pass 1's changes have cleared the ghost gate yet, so none are eligible for live consideration regardless.

stage_disposition_pass_3.stage_-1_read_ledger: DONE -- full pass 1/pass 2 ledger read before any action this pass, including cross-checking pass-1's 8 warrants against the actual current source (all 8 confirmed present, plus WARRANT#9 and EA_BUILD_ID/Lesson#4 from pass 2) so this pass started from an accurate baseline rather than trusting the ledger's status fields blindly
stage_disposition_pass_3.stage_0_instrument_profiling: NOT RE-RUN this pass -- the existing EURUSD 121,026-bar profile (0xF115) was read and reused; no fresh profiling done for USDJPY (the instrument of the live calibration referenced in the mail) since that requires a live MarketInfo/tick session or a USDJPY .hst this environment does not have on hand. Flagged as a gap: the mail's mandate explicitly wants Stage 0 to re-verify per-instrument since 'all nine [lenses] may differ on USDJPY' -- not done.
stage_disposition_pass_3.stage_1_reachability_clamp_audit: NOT RE-RUN -- pass 3 did a targeted read (hypothesis #4) of 2 specific functions rather than a full-file static sweep. The 18-gate table from pass 1 was not re-audited for drift.
stage_disposition_pass_3.stage_2_threshold_legality: N/A this pass -- no new ILLEGAL_ABSOLUTE-class gates were the target; this pass's defect class is POWER_FLOOR, not SCALE/legality.
stage_disposition_pass_3.stage_4_learning_integrity: DONE for the specific gap targeted -- WARRANT#10/#11 close the two sibling gaps to the n>=500 floor established pass 2. The primary Phase 3B search's own multiple-testing gap (hypothesis #3) remains open, as does any possibility of further siblings not yet found (Phase 3B.7 blend-ratio calibration was read but not exhaustively audited for the same defect class).

stage_disposition_pass_3.stage_7_orthogonal_validation: NOT DONE -- same structural gap as every prior pass: requires a real calibration/backtest run this environment cannot execute standalone (needs Manuel to attach SLS(31) to a live/demo chart).

did_this_pass_improve_the_agent.verdict: MIXED, HONESTLY REPORTED -- not COMPETENCE-FLAT (real work shipped and real defects found), but not fully validated either
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent.what_actually_improved: 8 illegal-absolute gates converted to legal forms with filed warrants (Stage 2, complete); one real second-normalisation-of-the-same-quantity bug found and fixed (W7, PARITY-class); one real comment/value-scale bug found (W2/Lesson#1); one real version-identity bug found and NOT yet fixed (Lesson#4); one significant, real, unfixed Power Doctrine gap found and correctly NOT papered over (hypothesis #2, the n<500 floor)
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent.what_did_not_improve: zero warrants have been measured against a real backtest -- prediction_MAE, CI coverage, and directional accuracy (SS-II.4) are all still N/A because nothing has a measured_effect yet. This pass produced hypotheses and warrants, not validated competence.
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent.regression_rate: 0/8 shipped-and-reverted, but per SS-II.5 that is not yet meaningful (nothing has been tested against reality to revert from)
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent.honest_summary: This pass did real Stage 0-2 engineering grounded in real data and real code, and surfaced one significant, previously-undiscovered defect (the Power Doctrine gap) that the rest of this very protocol was written to catch -- finding it and NOT quietly fixing it without a warrant is itself evidence the discipline is working. But calling this pass 'complete' would be false: Stages 6-8 (the parts that actually prove any of this helped) never ran, because the one live calibration attempt was cut short (partly by my own tooling colliding with it) and was not restarted before the user asked to finish. Pass 2 (compile + validated real backtest) is accordingly NOT started.

did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3.verdict: MIXED, HONESTLY REPORTED -- same shape as pass 1 and pass 2: real defect found and fixed, zero validation against reality yet
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3.what_actually_improved: Confirmed pass-1/pass-2 warrants (1-9) are genuinely present in the compiled SLS(30) baseline, not just claimed by the ledger -- a real accuracy check on the ledger itself, not just on TUNGSTEN. Found and fixed a real, live-consequential Power Doctrine gap (Lesson#5's own point: fixing one instance of a Standing Prohibition violation does not fix the class) in two sibling subsystems WARRANT#9 never touched. Correctly declined to attempt the full five-message mandate in one pass, and instead of silently dropping the rest, wrote it into `mandate_roadmap_2026_07_10` so it survives as a concrete plan rather than requiring the next session to re-read and re-triage five raw mail messages.
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3.what_did_not_improve: prediction_MAE/CI-coverage/directional-accuracy remain N/A after 3 passes and 11 total warrants -- the project has never once closed the predict-then-measure loop SS-II.4 exists for. This is now the single largest, most overdue gap: not a missing feature, but a missing validation cycle. Also: pass 3 did not re-run Stage 0 profiling on the instrument the mail's own numbers reference (USDJPY), so this pass's fix, while clearly correct by the Power Doctrine's own math, was not cross-checked against the specific 65k-bar dataset the mail cites.
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3.regression_rate: 0/11 shipped-and-reverted -- still not meaningful (SS-II.5's own point: a regression_rate of exactly 0 after 3 passes is itself a flag, not a triumph, given nothing has ever been measured against reality to revert from)
did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3.honest_summary: Three passes in, TUNGSTEN's static/structural discipline (legality, power floors, version identity) is measurably better and independently re-verified each pass rather than taken on faith. But the entire tungsten-forge v2.0 thesis -- that predictions should be scored, competence should compound, and 'exponential improvement' should be a measured claim rather than rhetoric -- cannot yet be evaluated, because no pass has completed a real calibration/ghost cycle against its own shipped warrants. The single highest-leverage next action for ANY future pass, before adding more scope, is closing that loop: attach SLS(31) to USDJPY M5 (or EURUSD M5, matching the instrument this session's own profile is grounded in) and run a full calibration through to a ghost verdict, then come back and score WARRANT#9/#10/#11 against what actually happened. Everything else -- the win-intelligence engine, the nine lenses, reverse-mining, retrospective intelligence -- is real, well-specified future work, but building more on an unvalidated foundation repeats pass 2's own diagnosis of Phase 3B: a lot of activity without knowing whether any of it survives contact with real data.

mandate_roadmap_2026_07_10.source: 5-message relay mailbox directive stack, 2026-07-10T08:19-08:46Z, archived in the relay mailbox under _archive/. TUNGSTEN_EDGE_DOCTRINE.md (final version) installed at ~/.claude/skills/tungsten-forge/EDGE_DOCTRINE.md as a permanent companion thesis, per the mail's own instruction.
mandate_roadmap_2026_07_10.purpose: So a future pass reads this table instead of re-deriving scope from five long raw messages. Each row is a candidate single-pass scope, per this project's own 'never batch subsystems' practice. Status reflects this pass (pass 3) only.

items.item: Win-Intelligence W-I.1 Edge Extraction Engine (search exit/target/horizon/partial-scaling space per confirmed edge, walk-forward validated)
items.note: requires a working exit/geometry backtest harness this environment does not yet have; likely needs its own infrastructure warrant before the search itself

TUNGSTEN's existing position-sizing code was not audited this pass to see what sizing logic already exists vs. what the mail assumes is missing -- first step for a future pass is that audit, not a fresh build

items.item: Win-Intelligence W-I.3 Deeper Edge Discovery (triple interactions, session/regime-transition/agreement-state conditioning)
items.status: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED as a side effect
items.note: WARRANT#10/#11 don't add new conditioning dimensions, but they do make the EXISTING session/volatility conditioning honest about when it has enough data to trust -- a prerequisite for W-I.3, not W-I.3 itself

items.item: Win-Intelligence W-I.4 Edge Ensemble (combine weakly-correlated confirmed edges)
items.note: blocked on having 2+ independently-confirmed edges to combine, which this project does not yet have (the only 'confirmed' edge referenced anywhere is from the mail's own external analysis, not yet independently reproduced in this environment)

items.item: Win-Intelligence W-I.5 Meta-Learning (prioritise search toward historically-productive hypothesis classes)
items.note: the forge_ledger.json hypotheses table IS the substrate this would read from -- feasible once there are enough CONFIRMED/REFUTED hypotheses to show a pattern (currently 4 hypotheses total, too few)

items.item: Win-Intelligence W-I.6 Real-Time Opportunity Recognition (press hard on strongly-replicated matches, stand aside otherwise)
items.note: depends on W-I.1-W-I.4 existing first; this is a consumer of the other engines, not independently buildable

WARRANT#10 makes the existing session-conditioning subsystem honest about power; did not add per-session reporting to the honesty dashboard (no such dashboard exists yet in this codebase -- flagged below)

items.item: Edge Doctrine Lens 2 -- Exit geometry search
Edge Doctrine Lens 3 -- Avoidance (hunt losing populations explicitly)
items.note: unknown whether TUNGSTEN's existing code has an explicit avoidance/anti-edge model or only ever scores toward taking trades -- first step is an audit, not a build

items.item: Edge Doctrine Lens 4 -- Asymmetry over win-rate (and the later Addendum A correction: win-rate as read-quality, not opposed to edge)
items.note: requires the honesty dashboard (win-rate + expectancy + geometry reported together) the mail describes -- no such dashboard exists in TUNGSTEN's code yet; building it is itself a reasonable single-pass scope
overlaps W-I.2; same next step (audit existing sizing code first)

items.item: Edge Doctrine Lens 6 -- Opportunity cost / coverage
items.note: the mail's reverse-mining addendum's own coverage claim (48% vs 9%) has not been independently reproduced against this environment's EURUSD data

the file's session/volatility/regime multi-dimensional weight structure IS an interaction search in spirit; not audited this pass for whether it actually captures the mail's specific low-disp x high-vol interaction claim

items.item: Edge Doctrine Lens 8 -- Adaptation speed / decay monitoring
items.note: unknown whether TUNGSTEN has any live decay-vs-CI monitor for deployed weights; first step is an audit
the deepest, least code-mechanical lens -- likely a hypothesis-generation exercise (name candidate forced-flow footprints, test each under full discipline) rather than a single warrant

items.item: Reverse-mining discovery engine (mine winning trades backward for shared pre-conditions)
items.note: a genuinely new subsystem, not an extension of existing code -- the mail's own 48%-coverage/4-of-4-replication claim for this technique has not been independently reproduced here

items.item: Retrospective Intelligence (Addendum B, six-question post-period self-review reflex)
items.note: requires the six-question analysis to run against a real trade record, which requires trades to have actually happened (calibration/live), which per did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3 above hasn't happened yet for ANY pass's changes

items.item: Honesty dashboard (recurring theme across every doctrine addendum -- win-rate+expectancy+geometry, extraction efficiency, ensemble return, survival margin, meta-learning yield, per-lens contribution, six-question review summary)
items.note: flagged here because it is a SHARED dependency of at least 5 other roadmap items above -- likely the single highest-leverage next scope after closing the pass-1/2/3 validation loop, since almost everything else wants to report through it

mandate_roadmap_2026_07_10.recommended_next_pass_scope: Per did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_3's own conclusion: before adding any new roadmap item above, close the validation loop on WARRANT#9/#10/#11 with a real calibration run. If a future pass must add new scope instead, the honesty dashboard is the best-leveraged single choice since so many roadmap items depend on it existing.
